title: ‘Mank’ Is Here. Does David Fincher’s Movie Live Up to the Hype?
format: Sean and Amanda talk through David Fincher's 'Mank': the backstory, the black-and-white choice, the cast, the phony Orson Welles controversy. Then they handicap a 2020 Best Director race almost nobody has seen yet.

films.title: Mank
films.note: Fincher's 11th feature, from a script by his late father Jack, about Herman Mankiewicz writing 'Citizen Kane.' Fincher first tried to make it in the late '90s with Kevin Spacey and Jodie Foster; Netflix finally bankrolled it. Sean's most-anticipated movie in years; both say it clicked on a second viewing.

films.title: Citizen Kane
films.note: The text 'Mank' is in dialogue with, structurally and stylistically. They recommend a rewatch, and cite Pauline Kael's 'Raising Kane' as the disputed source for the Mankiewicz-vs-Welles authorship question.

films.title: The Social Network
films.note: Sean's twin to 'Mank': same contempt for people who don't understand the power they wield, same willingness to bend fact for a good dramatic beat. Also the Fincher/Sorkin echo, with Jack Fincher now up against Sorkin at the Oscars.

films.title: Zodiac
films.note: Held up as the Fincher with the great first five minutes, and possibly his masterpiece; a movie they think would land bigger in the Oscar conversation now than it did in 2007.

films.title: Gone Girl
films.note: Ranked above 'Mank' by both; cited for the kind of slick modern Fincher 'Mank' deliberately is not.

films.title: The Curious Case of Benjamin Button
films.note: The emotional Fincher comparison, made not long after Jack Fincher's death, and the San Simeon walk recalls its piazza sequence.

films.title: Fight Club
films.note: Sean would still take its anarchy over 'Mank'; Amanda is the lesser fan.

films.title: Se7en
films.note: The Fincher breakout that put a chip on his shoulder in the mid-'90s. 'There are no serial killers in Mank, I promise you.'

films.title: The Game
films.note: Around when Fincher first wanted to make 'Mank.'

films.title: Alien 3
films.note: The early-career struggle that preceded 'Se7en.'

films.title: Darkest Hour
films.note: Gary Oldman's actual Oscar win, which they call peak Oscar bait, versus the fuller inhabiting they see in his Mankiewicz.

films.title: Mean Girls
films.note: Amanda Seyfried's calling card; a critic's note that she'd be the first 'Mean Girls' Oscar winner if 'Mank' lands it.

segments.heading: The 2020 awards race
segments.note: handicapping Best Director off the Gold Derby odds

films.title: Nomadland
films.note: Chloé Zhao neck-and-neck with Fincher at the top of the Best Director odds; the contrast they keep coming back to.

films.title: Minari
films.note: Lee Isaac Chung; loved out of Sundance but barely seen, like most of the field.

films.title: Ma Rainey's Black Bottom
films.note: George C. Wolfe directing a play well, but not where they expect the narrative to land.

films.title: Da 5 Bloods
films.note: Spike Lee, who still has never won Best Director despite the 'BlacKkKlansman' screenplay win.

films.title: The Father
films.note: Florian Zeller; Sean saw it at Sundance and is lukewarm, though Anthony Hopkins is a Best Actor threat.

films.title: News of the World
films.note: Paul Greengrass in classical old-Hollywood mode; Sean thinks it plays.

films.title: The Trial of the Chicago 7
films.note: Aaron Sorkin — the perverse prospect of him competing against Fincher's father for screenplay.

films.title: One Night in Miami
films.note: Regina King; another well-directed play.

films.title: Judas and the Black Messiah
films.note: Shaka King, a 100-to-1 long shot nobody has seen yet.

films.title: The United States vs. Billie Holiday
films.note: Lee Daniels, the other unseen 100-to-1 entry.

films.title: Soul
films.note: Reznor and Ross's other 2020 score, raising the odds of a double nomination.

referenced.group: Old Hollywood the writers came from
